title: Digital Marketing
tagline: Help African businesses grow online. Every company needs this skill.
capstone_project: Run a real 30-day marketing campaign for a Kigali small business (free certifications from Google + HubSpot)

modules.title: Social Media Strategy — Not Just Posting
modules.why_africa: Every small business in Kigali has a WhatsApp and a Facebook page. Very few know how to use them strategically. A digital marketer who can turn likes into customers is worth their weight in gold.
concepts: The difference between content and strategy
Choosing the right platforms for African audiences

video_primary.title: Social Media Marketing Full Course
video_primary.watch_for: Content pillars — the 4-5 topics your brand talks about. This is the foundation of every successful social media account.

practice_exercise.title: Social Media Audit of a Kigali Business
practice_exercise.instructions: Pick a real Kigali business (a restaurant, a salon, a boutique). Review their Instagram and Facebook pages. Write a 1-page audit: (1) What they are doing well, (2) What is missing, (3) 5 specific post ideas for the next week, (4) Best posting times based on when their audience is active. Share this audit with them — this is how you get your first freelance client.

interview_questions.question: What is the difference between organic and paid social media?
interview_questions.model_answer: Organic reach is free — you post content and hope the algorithm shows it to your followers. Paid is when you give the platform money to show your content to a specific audience. Organic builds trust over time. Paid scales fast but costs money. Professional marketers use both: organic for community, paid for new customer acquisition.

modules.portfolio_contribution: Your first social media audit case study

modules.title: SEO Basics — Getting Found on Google
modules.why_africa: When a tourist searches 'Kigali gorilla tour', the companies that appear first get the bookings. SEO is how you earn that placement. It is free traffic that compounds over time.

video_primary.title: SEO Tutorial for Beginners (Complete Course)
video_primary.watch_for: Keyword research — this is the foundation. If you pick the wrong keywords, nothing else matters.

practice_exercise.title: Keyword Research for a Rwandan Business
practice_exercise.instructions: Use Google's free tools (Google Trends, 'Searches related to', keyword suggestions in search bar) to find 20 keywords a Kigali coffee shop could target. Group them into: high-intent (people ready to buy), informational (people learning), local (people searching for location). Write a 1-page SEO recommendation.

interview_questions.question: What is the difference between on-page SEO and off-page SEO?
interview_questions.model_answer: On-page SEO is what you do on your own website — titles, headings, content quality, internal links. You control it completely. Off-page SEO is what happens outside your site — other sites linking to you (backlinks), mentions, reviews. You influence it but do not fully control it. Both matter: on-page is the foundation, off-page is the trust signal.

modules.title: Google Analytics — Measuring What Works
modules.why_africa: Marketing without measurement is guessing. Google Analytics is free, universal, and required by every marketing job. The Google Analytics certification is free and globally recognised.

video_primary.title: Google Analytics 4 Tutorial for Beginners
video_primary.watch_for: Setting up conversion events. This is the #1 thing employers test.

practice_exercise.title: Earn Your Google Analytics Certification
practice_exercise.instructions: Go to skillshop.exceedlms.com and take the free Google Analytics certification. This is a real, globally recognised credential. Add it to your LinkedIn when you pass.

interview_questions.question: What is a bounce rate and what is a good one?
interview_questions.model_answer: Bounce rate is the percentage of visitors who leave after viewing only one page. 'Good' depends on the page type. For a blog post, 70-90% can be normal — they read and leave. For a product page, above 50% is bad — visitors are not clicking through. Context matters more than the raw number.

modules.title: Content Creation that Converts
modules.why_africa: Content is how Africans discover new products. But generic content fails. Content that speaks to African contexts, uses local languages, and solves local problems — that is what converts.

video_primary.title: Content Marketing Full Course
video_primary.watch_for: The buyer journey framework — every piece of content should target one stage.

practice_exercise.title: Create a 1-Week Content Calendar
practice_exercise.instructions:
Pick a real or imagined Rwandan business. Create 7 posts for the week:
- 2 awareness posts (educational)
- 2 consideration posts (product benefits)
- 1 decision post (offer/promotion)
- 1 customer story
- 1 behind-the-scenes

Design each with Canva (free). Include captions, hashtags, and best posting time. Save as a 1-page PDF content calendar.

interview_questions.question: How do you measure the success of a content campaign?
interview_questions.model_answer: Depends on the goal. For awareness: reach, impressions, video views. For consideration: time on page, bounce rate, engagement. For decision: clicks, sign-ups, sales. Vanity metrics like likes are not success — pick 2-3 metrics that tie directly to business outcomes.

modules.title: Email Marketing
modules.why_africa: Email is the highest ROI marketing channel — $42 back for every $1 spent. It is owned media (no algorithm blocks you). Every business needs email marketing and very few Rwandan companies do it well.
concepts: Metrics: open rate, click rate, unsubscribes

video_primary.title: Email Marketing Crash Course
video_primary.watch_for: Subject lines — the entire email's success depends on the first 5 words.

practice_exercise.title: Write a 5-Email Welcome Sequence
practice_exercise.instructions:
For a fictional Kigali tour company, write 5 welcome emails sent over 10 days to new subscribers:
1. Day 1: Welcome + what to expect
2. Day 3: Our story + why we do this
3. Day 5: Top 5 things tourists miss in Rwanda
4. Day 7: Customer story
5. Day 10: Special welcome offer

Each email: compelling subject line, clear call to action, readable length. Save as a Google Doc.

interview_questions.question: What is email segmentation and why does it matter?
interview_questions.model_answer: Segmentation means dividing your email list into groups based on behaviour, demographics, or interests — then sending each group different content. Segmented emails get 14% higher open rates and 100% higher click rates. Instead of blasting everyone the same message, you send relevant messages. This respects the reader's time.

modules.title: Paid Advertising Basics
modules.why_africa: Facebook and Google ads are how most African e-commerce grows. A marketer who can run profitable ad campaigns is immediately more valuable than one who cannot.
concepts: Targeting — who you show ads to
Ad creative — what makes people click

video_primary.title: Facebook Ads Tutorial for Beginners
video_primary.watch_for: The targeting section — bad targeting burns money. Good targeting is everything.

practice_exercise.title: Plan a 10,000 RWF Ad Campaign
practice_exercise.instructions:
You have 10,000 RWF to spend on Facebook Ads to promote a Kigali bakery's new service. Plan:
1. Your objective (awareness, traffic, or conversions)
2. Your target audience (age, location, interests)
3. Your ad creative (image description + caption)
4. Your budget split across 3 days
5. How you will measure success

This is exactly the planning you do on day one of any paid ads job.

interview_questions.question: What is ROAS?
interview_questions.model_answer: ROAS is Return On Ad Spend. For every 1 RWF spent on ads, how many RWF came back in revenue. A ROAS of 3 means you made 3 RWF for every 1 RWF spent. Different businesses need different ROAS targets — a low-margin grocery might need ROAS of 8. A high-margin SaaS might be fine with ROAS of 2.

modules.title: Build Your Portfolio with a Real Business
modules.why_africa: Portfolio beats resume. Find a real small business in Kigali that needs help, offer to work free for 30 days, document the results. Now you have a case study no competitor has.

video_primary.title: How to Get Your First Marketing Client
video_primary.watch_for: The 'offer value first' approach — this is how you get clients without experience.

practice_exercise.title: 30-Day Real Business Campaign
practice_exercise.instructions:
1. Find a small business near you that has a weak social media presence (salon, shop, restaurant)
2. Offer to run their social media for 30 days, free, in exchange for permission to use the results as a case study
3. Audit their current state (screenshots!)
4. Plan and post 3x per week for 30 days
5. Track: followers gained, engagement, any sales they credit to your work
6. Write a case study: before, after, what you did, what worked, what you would do next

This is a real portfolio piece that beats any theory.

interview_questions.question: Tell me about a campaign you ran.
interview_questions.model_answer: Structure: (1) The client and goal, (2) What you inherited (baseline), (3) What you did and why, (4) The numbers (before/after), (5) What you learned. Never exaggerate. Never show vanity metrics without business metrics.

modules.portfolio_contribution: Real 30-day campaign case study — your most valuable portfolio piece

modules.title: Getting Clients as a Freelancer
modules.why_africa: The highest paying digital marketing work is remote freelancing for international clients. African marketers earn 3-5x more through international freelance than local jobs.

video_primary.title: How to Get Started on Upwork
video_primary.watch_for: The first 10 proposals are the hardest. Expect rejection. Keep applying.

practice_exercise.title: Launch Your Freelance Profile
practice_exercise.instructions:
1. Create an Upwork account
2. Write a profile headline targeting one niche (e.g., 'Facebook Ads for African E-commerce')
3. Write an 'About' section with your case study from Module 7
4. Add portfolio items with screenshots
5. Submit 10 proposals this week
6. Also create a LinkedIn profile optimised for marketing roles

Expect 0-1 wins from first 10 proposals. Do not stop.

interview_questions.question: Why should we hire you instead of a cheaper freelancer?
interview_questions.model_answer: Not about price. About results. Show a specific case study: 'For X business, I ran a 30-day campaign that grew followers from 400 to 1,200 and drove Y sales. I bring that kind of thinking.' Specifics beat generalities every time.

modules.portfolio_contribution: Live Upwork + LinkedIn profile — your gateway to international clients
